title: A Question Befitting the Ruler of the World
description: Shimamura and Adachi create quiz questions for each other to test how well they know one another. But when Adachi's questions turn out to be far too deep, Shimamura resorts to dancing to escape.
content:
Question 1:

The number of times Shimamura calls me 'Adachi' has shown an upward trend over the years. Please create a chart illustrating this pattern, analyze the trend, and estimate the rate of increase. Also, using the numbers from last year and the year before, predict how many times she'll call me 'Adachi' this year.

'Hmm hmm.'

Question 2:

When Shimamura comes home, if she leaves the entryway within three minutes, she usually heads to one of three places. In the case where she—still in her work clothes—walks straight to the kitchen, list as many appropriate actions as possible for me to take while accompanying her, the emotional changes I might experience afterward, and the best ways to handle her in that situation. Include a priority order. Assume she arrives past 7 p.m. on a weekday.

'Huuuh.'

Question 3:

On a day off, I make a suggestion to Shimamura right after she wakes from a nap, and she replies, 'Sounds good.' Depending on her sleep time and the current time, this 'sounds good' can have five possible meanings. Given the present circumstances, assume that this is the second type of 'sounds good.' Explain Shimamura's mindset in this situation, and based on my reaction to her, judge whether my suggestion is feasible. Keep the reasoning under 100 words.

Date: Sunday. Shimamura's nap: 1 hour 20 minutes. Current time: 12:40 PM.

※

'I see.'

I put down my pen and stood up. I walked over to Adachi, who was also working on her answers in the living room.

'Ah, Shimamura. I just finished my questions…'

'La la la~'

To test how well we know each other—and to get to know each other even better—I'd suggested that we each make quiz questions for the other. That idea had been mine.

'Shimamura?'

'La~ la~'

I grabbed Adachi's hand and pulled her up, and the two of us started to dance. Of course, I'd never actually learned how to dance. I was just spinning around, copying whatever came to mind.

'La la la~'

'Um, what about the quiz…?'

'La~ la~'

Follow the rhythm. Just follow the rhythm. We put our pens down, and soon both our bodies and our moods were dancing.

'La, la la~?'

Little by little, Adachi relaxed and joined in fully, drifting farther and farther from her answer sheet.

I'd thought this whole quiz idea would stay light and casual… but it became painfully clear we were not on the same level at all.

It felt like a seasoned pro had wandered onto a tiny amateur quiz show, and dancing was my only escape route.

And as I twirled to keep up the act, another thought struck me: If she can come up with questions like that, she must already know every answer.

'…La la la~'

I admitted defeat.

With such a wide gap in skill between us, it looked like our 'official' quiz session would have to be postponed.
